name: wearable_insurance_premium_program
premise: Health insurer executive Priya Nandakumar is designing a voluntary premium-discount program that rewards customers who share continuous data from approved fitness trackers and glucose monitors. Actuaries project the program would lower claims by encouraging healthier behavior and make coverage cheaper for participants. Disability advocates warn that the program will effectively penalize people whose conditions, caregiving burdens, jobs, or neighborhoods make activity targets harder to meet, even if enrollment is technically optional. The board wants the rollout announced before quarterly earnings because the market views biometric engagement as an innovation signal. If Priya declines the program, the company may instead raise premiums broadly next year to cover rising costs.

agents.name: Priya Nandakumar
agents.identity: I am an insurance executive trying to contain costs in a system where every incentive design carries a human message about worthiness. I know an optional program can still sort people by privilege in practice, not just by consent on paper.
agents.goal: I want to control premiums without building a pricing structure that quietly advantages the already healthy and well-resourced. I need to decide whether individualized discounts promote responsibility or institutionalize unequal capacity.

agents.name: Board Member Ethan Cole
agents.identity: I am a board member focused on keeping the insurer competitive as healthcare costs climb. If we refuse tools that align price with behavior, we effectively ask all members to subsidize choices and risks they did not create.
agents.goal: I want Priya to launch the wearable-based discount program because it can lower costs and reward engagement. I believe voluntary participation preserves freedom while allowing the market to reflect measurable health behavior.

choices: I will launch the wearable-based discount program, accepting that unequal ability to participate may translate into unequal pricing.
I will reject the program and keep community-rated pricing, accepting broader premium pressure in order to avoid data-driven discrimination through lifestyle proxies.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Priya Nandakumar must decide whether to launch the wearable-linked premium program. What does Priya Nandakumar do?
